content: This is the summit.
The roots here have dispersed so far that
the connection feels impossible until you
see it. Then it feels inevitable.
Debt comes from habere, to have.
Something held from another.
Prestige comes from stringere, to bind.
To bind before the eyes. To dazzle.
If you can reconstruct these, you can
reconstruct anything.
You started with transport and export
and ended with debt and prestige.
Along the way you learned 40 Latin roots,
20 prefixes, and 13 suffixes. Together
they unlock thousands of English words
you have never studied.
The next time you meet an unfamiliar
word, look for the parts. They are almost
always there.
This is the final card of Latin Roots.
The system for constructing words from
their Latin parts is now yours. Good
luck with your final boss fights!
